mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations and force equity portfolio de-risking over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where unemployment exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer spending strength that drives corporate revenue growth and supports positive equity market performance over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio on 20-day forward returns over a 24-month sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates manufacturing contraction that precedes earnings downgrades and triggers short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production growth yields positive 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of observations during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPI exceeds a 2.5 percent annualized threshold, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and suppresses equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of months where CPI growth surpasses the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative information coefficient over rolling 3-year windows or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent during a post-shock recovery phase confirms economic stabilization that removes tail-risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 momentum over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5 percent in recovery regimes yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.0 percent, liquidity conditions become highly accommodative and trigger rapid risk-on positioning that pushes equity prices higher within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of periods where the funds rate drops below 2.0 percent.
